zh.lifeSummary: 當William Carlton於1538年出生在英國越可爾衛克菲德時，他的父親William Carlton Sr41歲，母親Mrs William Carlton Sr40歲。他於1561年6月22日與Joan Margaret Rippingale在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾朗 班寧頓結婚。他們至少是4個女兒的父母。他去世於1583年8月31日在英國米德萊可倫敦享年45歲。

nameMeanings.name: Carlton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (northern and eastern England): habitational name from any of various places called Carlton (Bedfordshire, Cambridgeshire, Durham, Lincolnshire, Leicestershire, Nottinghamshire, Northamptonshire, Suffolk, East Yorkshire, Yorkshire, and North Yorkshire) or Carleton (Cumberland, Lancashire, Norfolk, Yorkshire). All come from Old Norse karl ‘common man, peasant’ + Old English tūn ‘settlement’ (compare Charlton ). Places spelled Carl(e)ton (as opposed to Charlton) are in areas of Scandinavian settlement, mostly in northern England.

variants.name: Carleton
meanings: English (northern and eastern England): variant of   Carlton  .
Irish (Ulster): adopted in Northern Ireland as an Anglicized form of Ó Cairealláin, see Carolan 2.

variants.name: Charlton
meanings: English (Northumberland and Durham): habitational name from North Charlton or Charlton near Bellingham (both Northumberland) or from any of the many other places so named (there are over ninety examples in England), from Old English Ceorlatūn ‘settlement (Old English tūn) of the peasants’. Old English ceorl denoted originally a free peasant of the lowest rank, later (but probably already before the Norman conquest) a tenant in pure villeinage, a serf or bondsman.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

en.lifeSummary: When William Carlton was born in 1538, in Wakefield, Yorkshire, England, his father, William Carlton Sr, was 41 and his mother, Mrs William Carlton Sr, was 40. He married Joan Margaret Rippingale on 22 June 1561, in Long Bennington, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 daughters. He died on 31 August 1583, in London, Middlesex, England, at the age of 45.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William Carlton est né en 1538, à Wakefield, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, William Carlton Sr, avait 41 ans et sa mère, Mrs William Carlton Sr, avait 40 ans. Il a épousé Joan Margaret Rippingale le 22 juin 1561, à Long Bennington, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 filles. Il est décédé le 31 août 1583, à Londres, Middlesex, Angleterre, à l’âge de 45 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: William Carlton(이)가 1538년 영국 요르크실르 웨이크필드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Carlton Sr(은)는 41세였고, 그의 어머니 Mrs William Carlton Sr(은)는 40세였다. 그는 1561년 6월 22일 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 론그 벤잉턴에서 Joan Margaret Rippingale(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1583년 8월 31일 영국 미들레세스 런던에서 45세의 나이로 사망했다.

Он женился на женщине по имени Joan Margaret Rippingale 22 июня 1561 г. в местности лонг беннингтон, линколншир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 дочери. Он умер 31 августа 1583 г. в местности Лондон, миддлесекс, Англия в возрасте 45 лет.
